title: EASA Part-IS Information Security Management System (ISMS) audit-evidence register and scope coverage - IS.OR.200 + IS.OR.245 cross-organisation roll-up
exclusions: Excludes ISMS scope items in pre-22-February-2026 transitional period (operator may elect to be audited only against the legacy regime until effective date). Excludes non-EU/EEA approved organisations that have no EASA Part-IS obligation.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.60.14` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `do326a_clause="EASA-Part-IS-IS-OR-200"`. Every row pins (organisation_id, approval_type, isms_scope_item, control_status, last_evidence_refresh, days_since_refresh, retention_due_date, audit_finding_count, severity, risk_score).

compliance.assurance_rationale: EASA Part-IS IS.OR.200 requires every approved organisation to establish, implement, maintain, and continuously improve an ISMS that covers information and systems with potential safety impact. This UC implements the ISMS scope register and the per-control evidence freshness tracker.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that the operator's ISMS has a documented scope, that each control has fresh evidence on file, that audit findings are tracked to closure, and that record-keeping meets the IS.OR.245 5-year retention.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search archived to `audit_evidence`, the `ISMS Health` dashboard, the ISMS scope document, the internal-audit findings register.

compliance.assurance_rationale: EASA Part-IS IS.OR.245 requires a 5-year retention for ISMS records. This UC tracks the retention-due dates and surfaces records approaching disposal that may still be in-scope for audit.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Same as the primary clause, plus the `audit_evidence` index retention configuration.

compliance.assurance_rationale: EASA Part-IS IS.OR.205 requires annual ISMS risk-assessment review; this UC surfaces controls whose evidence has not been refreshed within 12 months.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidences that risk-assessment evidence is refreshed annually.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DO-326A §4.2 (Security Effectiveness Demonstration) requires that countermeasures be verified to be effective and to remain effective in service. The per-control evidence freshness register, paired with the audit-findings register and the annual refresh cadence, is the operator-side evidence that countermeasures continue to perform as intended; without this register §4.2 cannot be demonstrated to an auditor.
compliance.controlObjective: Operationalises §4.2 by tracking, per control, whether the operator can demonstrate continued effectiveness of the countermeasure with current evidence.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Allow an IS.OR.200 control's evidence refresh to age past 12 months without a renewed attestation. UC-22.60.14 MUST flag within 24 hours with risk_score >= 80.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Complete an annual evidence refresh for every IS.OR.200 control with current attestation and zero open audit findings. UC-22.60.14 MUST record `control_status=current` and `risk_score=0` for that control.

dataSources: KV Store `easa_part_is_isms_register_lookup` (organisation_id, approval_type - Part-21 / Part-145 / Part-CAMO / Part-147 / Part-ORA / Part-OPS / Part-ATM, isms_scope_item, control_id, control_status, last_evidence_refresh, evidence_ref, retention_due_date). KV Store `easa_part_is_audit_findings_lookup` (organisation_id, finding_id, finding_status, days_open).
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup easa_part_is_isms_register_lookup
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval days_since_refresh = case(isnotnull(last_evidence_refresh), round((now_ts - last_evidence_refresh) / 86400, 1), 1=1, null)
| eval days_to_retention = case(isnotnull(retention_due_date), round((retention_due_date - now_ts) / 86400, 1), 1=1, null)
| join type=outer organisation_id [
    | inputlookup easa_part_is_audit_findings_lookup
    | where finding_status IN ("open", "in-progress")
    | stats count AS audit_finding_count max(days_open) AS oldest_open_finding by organisation_id ]
| fillnull audit_finding_count value=0
| eval control_status = case(
    isnull(last_evidence_refresh), "missing",
    days_since_refresh > 365, "expired",
    days_since_refresh > 335, "renewal-due",
    1=1, "current")
| eval risk_score = case(
    control_status = "missing", 90,
    control_status = "expired" AND audit_finding_count > 0, 95,
    control_status = "expired", 80,
    control_status = "renewal-due" AND audit_finding_count > 0, 70,
    control_status = "renewal-due", 40,
    audit_finding_count > 0 AND oldest_open_finding > 90, 60,
    days_to_retention <= 14 AND control_status = "current", 30,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| eval severity = case(risk_score >= 80, "critical", risk_score >= 60, "high", 1=1, "medium")
| sort 0 -risk_score, +days_since_refresh
| table organisation_id approval_type isms_scope_item control_id control_status last_evidence_refresh days_since_refresh retention_due_date days_to_retention audit_finding_count oldest_open_finding severity risk_score
description: Reconciles every IS.OR.200 ISMS scope item across the operator's approved organisations (Part-21 design, Part-145 maintenance, Part-CAMO continuing airworthiness, Part-147 training, Part-ORA aerodrome, Part-OPS air operations) against the evidence refresh cadence and open audit findings. Surfaces missing or expired evidence, controls with open audit findings, and records approaching the IS.OR.245 5-year retention disposal date.
value: Once EASA Part-IS becomes effective on 22 February 2026, NCAs will audit the ISMS as part of the standard organisation audit cycle. Missing evidence, expired attestations, or open findings will result in audit findings against the organisation approval, which can lead to conditioned or suspended approvals (Part-OPS AOC, Part-145 organisation approval). This UC closes the gap before the NCA auditor sees it.
implementation: (1) Populate `easa_part_is_isms_register_lookup` from the operator's GRC tool (ServiceNow GRC, RSA Archer, OneTrust) seeded from the IS.OR.250 Information Security Manual. (2) Populate `easa_part_is_audit_findings_lookup` from the internal-audit register. (3) Schedule UC-22.60.14 daily at 08:00 base-local; route `risk_score >= 80` to the DPO + CISO + Accountable Manager at severity P1, `risk_score 60-79` at severity P2. (4) Build the `ISMS Health` dashboard.
visualization: (Panel 1) Control status by approval-type (current / renewal-due / expired / missing). (Panel 2) Open audit findings by organisation. (Panel 3) Retention countdown heatmap. (Panel 4) 12-month evidence-refresh trend.

references.title: RTCA DO-326A - Airworthiness Security Process Specification

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Newly-added control mid-cycle**: a control added in response to a finding may be briefly missing refresh; allow 60 days for first refresh. 2. **Multi-organisation operator**: a Part-OPS operator with a Part-145 AMO and Part-CAMO subsidiary may have overlapping evidence; tag with `evidence_inherits_from` to avoid double-counting. 3. **Pre-effective-date scope**: organisations may be in transitional period; allow until 22-February-2026 grace.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `do326a_22_60_14_exceptions` with the DPO + CISO documented approval.
reviewer: Aviation Cybersecurity SME Panel + DPO + CISO + Accountable Manager
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-22.60.1 in production.
- KV Store `easa_part_is_isms_register_lookup`.
- KV Store `easa_part_is_audit_findings_lookup`.
- IS.OR.250 Information Security Manual under document control.

## Step 1 - ISMS register schema

```ini
[easa_part_is_isms_register_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.organisation_id = string
field.approval_type = string
field.isms_scope_item = string
field.control_id = string
field.control_status = string
field.last_evidence_refresh = time
field.evidence_ref = string
field.retention_due_date = time
```

## Step 2 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.60.14: ISMS Health]
cron_schedule = 0 8 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.60.14,reg=DO-326A,clause=EASA-Part-IS-IS-OR-200
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 3 - Runbooks

**`risk_score >= 80`** (owner: DPO + CISO + Accountable Manager, severity P1): Within 5 business days, the responsible control owner refreshes the evidence; the DPO reviews the open audit findings.

**`risk_score 60-79`** (owner: Control Owner, severity P2): Within 14 business days, refresh the evidence.

## Step 4 - Aviation-specific notes

- **Approval-type-specific controls**: Part-145 has different controls than Part-OPS; ensure the ISMS scope mapping reflects the approval type.
- **Contracted activity flow-down**: contracted parties (per IS.OR.235) may carry their own evidence; ensure inherited evidence is recorded.
- **NCA-specific transposition**: some NCAs may issue local interpretive guidance that adds or removes scope items.
grandmaExplanation: The European safety regulator requires airlines, maintenance shops, and design organisations to keep a record of how they protect their information. We watch that every required control has fresh paperwork and that nothing is overdue, so the regulator can audit at any time.
